user_message: Working on Fabrikam Residences's whitespace analysis. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:22:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:26:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:28:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-O trap (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-O failure mode (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-O -- Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
